notes: Did cp cudart.dll physxcudart_20.dll to get the game properly started. Playing for over 15 hours now and game crashes regularly on no specific action in-game. Sometimes no crashes for several hours and then suddenly it crashes. Sometimes more frequently, sometimes less. Always with the following two message windows "application has requested the Runtime to terminate it in an unusual way" and "__memmove_sse2_unaligned() Address = 0x....... (filename not found) ....". Game still enjoyable, unless it crashes during a larger mission part... had to play headstone mind like 3-4 times... till i managed to get through without the game crashing.

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: The game runs okay-ish with the default wined3d implementation, but I noticed it seemed way smoother after trying Gallium Nine. I went to the GN standalone repository (https://github.com/iXit/wine-nine-standalone) tried release 0.2 and followed the instructions. Basically, "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/8980/pfx ./nine-install.sh" on the extracted folder. To check if it installed correctly, I changed directory to bin64, since the prefix is 64-bit (although the game itself seems 32-bit -- dx9 and all) and did "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/8980/pfx wine ./ninewinecfg.exe.so". This opens a little GUI that confirmed I had all the necessary files ready to go. The game was started with parameters GALLIUM_HUD=fps %command% just for initial benchmarks. I changed WillowGame.ini just a tiny bit, following some recommendations from youtube channel LowSpecGamer. The main changes I remember were detailMode from 2 to 1 (not 0, like LowSpecGamer) and turning VSync on. With Vsync on, I get a really smooth 30fps+no tearing experience that I didn't have with wined3d. I can turn every single graphics setting high at 1600x900 except Depth of Field. This and Dynamic Shadows are the heaviest settings, but even my APU+Gallium9 were able to tank them at smooth framerate. If the mileage varies, I could definitely tune down some other settings. Being 30fps instead of 60 doesn't bother too much, but I won't try Vsync off right now (or further lowering things until I get a constant 60 with this poor game's optimization). I HIGHLY recommend going to Borderlands Wiki and doing some camera tweaks, like changing Field of View to 16:9 screens (110 or so angle).

title: Borderlands Game of the Year

notes:
Game works perfect, but not with wine dx9. Unfortunately game doesn't support DX11, so I figure I can install d9vk in proton prefix. After launching game for the first time (to create prefix) you should exit and do next:
$ export WINEPREFIX=/path_to_your_steam_library/steamapps/compatdata/8980/ 
$ setup_d9vk install
Please note that you should have system wine.
After install is complete, launch game again, and disable dynamic shadows (it doesn't have any problem but low fps, even on Windows). Now you'll get solid 40-60FPS almost all the time.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year

notes: Occasionally crashes due to a "Runtime Error, Pure Virtual Function Call R6025". I tried getting those probably missing DLL's via protontricks, but this didn't do the trick. Other workarounds like reinstalling the game's prerequisites(physx or vcredist) didn't help as well. Overall the performance is good, you can play it since you can ignore those error message for the first or second time in a row and just alt-tab yourself back into the game. If they appear a third time and you try alt-tab, you get a black game screen. So in the end, I stopped playing now(at lvl 55), because those crashes are too frustrating to grind for the achievements.

title: Borderlands Game of the Year

notes: Proton 4.11 doesn't work, process just die without any useful information. Build your own D9VK, don't forget build MinGW with dwarf2 extension. After this manipulation you will get solid 60 fps. Launch game with WINEDLLOVERRIDES=winedbg.exe=d %command%. If you play in coop, you will also need to install vcrun2008 from protontricks, or game will crash, after location change. There is also error messages: pure function error, after about 40-60 minutes of play. If you see this message, next area you will teleport may crash your game. Restart the game helps. It's safe to ignore this messages, but texture streaming will be disabled for some reason. If you try to press Ok button, game exit immediately.
title: Borderlands Game of the Year
